headline: Walmart and Amazon Explore Stablecoins. Visa Stock Falls but Analysts Don’t See Immediate Threat.
summary: Shares of  Visa  and  Mastercard  were sliding Friday after The Wall Street Journal reported that  Walmart  and  Amazon.com  among other merchants, have been considering issuing their own stablecoins to customers, a move that could siphon revenue from traditional payments providers.  Merchants’ decisions on whether to unveil stablecoins—cryptocurrencies pegged to stable currencies like the U.S. dollar—rest on the passage of the Genius Act, a bill moving through Congress that would regulate the coins and require issuers to maintain reserves of dollars or other liquid assets, the Journal said.
